_comment: Sequentia price server. It DISCOVERS your network's assets from the Asset Registry (ticker -> id), prices them from ONE market-data API, applies the admission rules below, and publishes the resulting fee-asset whitelist to your node via setfeeexchangerates (persist=false). You do NOT list assets here. These defaults point at the public Sequentia testnet demo, so a fresh install works out of the box; edit node_rpc to match your local node, and tune the rules in the config UI.

node_rpc._comment: Your LOCAL Sequentia node's RPC (where the whitelist is published). Use either user+password or a cookie file path.

source._comment: A Sequentia-format API returns every asset keyed by ticker ({"SEQ":{"price":..,"market_cap":..,"volume_24h":..}, ...}) in one call, so no JSON paths are needed. For a third-party API set format=custom and add price_path / market_cap_path / volume_24h_path (with an optional {ticker} placeholder).

feed_aliases._comment: Map a registry ticker to the key the price feed uses for that asset, when they differ. The Sequence token's registry/display ticker is tSEQ (TSEQ) but the feed prices it under the chain-independent key SEQ (as the node GUI and explorer also do), so they refer to the same asset and merge into one row.

default_thresholds._comment: Admission rules. Empty (only 'require') = admit every discovered asset that has a valid price. Add keys to tighten: min_market_cap, min_volume_24h, max_change_factor, max_volatility, min_price, max_price, issuer_domains. 'require' is 'all' or 'any'.

ui._comment: Web UI access. public_status / public_api expose the read-only price page and the JSON API (/api/prices, /api/whitelist) to anyone who can reach the UI port, rate-limited per IP. password_hash gates the admin area; set it from the Access tab or with --set-password (never write it by hand).
